sources.label: NHS error message guidance
borrowed: Explain what changed and what to do next in plain English.
Do not use inline validation-style error treatment for eligibility or recovery states.
rejected: Form-error red treatment as the dominant visual for whole-route booking recovery.

sources.label: NHS error summary guidance
borrowed: Keep one high-signal explanation block near the top of the current page.
Preserve focus discipline when surfacing recovery issues.
rejected: A generic 'There is a problem' heading for every booking recovery reason.

sources.label: NHS writing guidance
borrowed: Short sentences.
Direct next-step framing.
rejected: Any euphemistic or overly reassuring copy that hides unsettled booking truth.

sources.label: WAI alert pattern
borrowed: Non-focus-stealing status messaging for recovery announcements.

sources.label: WAI dialog modal pattern
borrowed: Return focus to the logical invoking point when a focused recovery affordance completes.
rejected: Escalating booking recovery into a modal dialog by default.

sources.label: WCAG 2.2 focus order
borrowed: Summary -> reason -> action -> return reading order.
rejected: Visual reordering that would break keyboard meaning.

sources.label: WCAG 2.2 timing adjustable
borrowed: Explicit expiry and pending-window language around time-bound recovery states.

sources.label: WCAG 2.2 status messages
borrowed: Polite live-region updates without stealing focus.
rejected: Relying only on visible copy updates for recovery state changes.

sources.label: Carbon status indicators
borrowed: Use text plus color plus shape instead of color alone.
Keep severity visible but calm.
rejected: Overloading the shell with multiple equal-weight warning badges.

sources.label: Carbon dialog pattern
borrowed: Single dominant action hierarchy.
rejected: Using dialogs for ordinary recovery explanations.

sources.label: Carbon read-only states
borrowed: Read-only remains readable and accessible when mutation is fenced.
rejected: Disabled-looking content for summary-safe recovery states.

sources.label: Linear redesign
borrowed: Reduced chrome noise so the main recovery content carries the hierarchy.
rejected: Dense navigation chrome competing with transactional recovery copy.

sources.label: Linear latest design refresh
borrowed: Structure should be felt, not loudly drawn.
Navigation surfaces should recede once the user is in the core task.
rejected: Extra separators or control rows that compete with recovery explanation.

sources.label: Playwright aria snapshots
borrowed: Use aria snapshots to lock the accessible recovery structure.
rejected: Relying only on screenshots for recovery regression proof.

sources.label: Playwright accessibility testing
borrowed: Pair automated scans with structural assertions.
rejected: Treating axe as the whole accessibility proof.

sources.label: Playwright trace viewer
borrowed: Capture trace zips for recovery flow debugging.
rejected: Relying on console logs instead of traces for route recovery debugging.
